q.en: Who first spotted gold at Sutter's Mill on January 24, 1848?
q.zh: 1848年1月24日，谁在萨特的磨坊最先发现了黄金？

options.en: Levi Strauss according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 李维·施特劳斯（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, "On the morning of January 24, 1848, James Marshall went to inspect the new mill... he spotted something shiny... It was gold!"
explain.zh: 书中写道：“1848年1月24日早晨，詹姆斯·马歇尔去检查新磨坊……他发现了一个闪亮的东西……那是黄金！

q.en: When gold was discovered, California belonged to Mexico. How soon after the discovery did Mexico sign a treaty giving California to the United States?
q.zh: 发现黄金时，加利福尼亚属于墨西哥。发现之后多久墨西哥签署了将加利福尼亚割让给美国的条约？

options.en: one year later according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一年之后（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states, "At the time of Marshall's discovery, California belonged to Mexico. Nine days later, Mexico signed a treaty that gave California to the United States."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“在马歇尔发现黄金时，加利福尼亚属于墨西哥。九天之后，墨西哥签署条约将加利福尼亚割让给美国。

q.en: The book says "placer mining" works because—
q.zh: 本书说“砂矿开采”之所以有效，是因为——

options.en: gold is much heavier than gravel, so it stays at the bottom of the pan
options.zh: 黄金比砾石重得多，所以会留在淘盘底部

options.en: gold is locked deep underground according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 黄金被深锁在地下（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book explains, "Placer mining works because gold is much heavier than gravel... The shiny flakes of gold stayed at the bottom of the pan."
explain.zh: 书中解释：“砂矿开采之所以有效，是因为黄金比砾石重得多……闪亮的金片会留在淘盘底部。

q.en: Why did Sam Brannan become rich from the gold rush without mining for gold himself?
q.zh: 为什么萨姆·布兰南没有亲自挖金却在淘金热中发了财？

options.en: He bought all the shovels in San Francisco and sold them at high prices
options.zh: 他买下旧金山所有的铲子并以高价卖出

options.en: He discovered the gold at Sutter's Mill according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他在萨特磨坊发现了黄金（按书中另一种说法）
他修建了跨阿拉斯加管道
他是加利福尼亚州州长

explain.en: The book says Brannan "bought all the shovels in San Francisco... then he filled a bottle with gold dust and ran through the streets yelling," selling expensive shovels.
explain.zh: 书中说布兰南“买下旧金山所有的铲子……然后装了一瓶金粉在街上边跑边喊”，以高价卖出铲子。

q.en: About how many people joined the gold rush in 1849 (the "forty-niners")?
q.zh: 1849年（即“四九年淘金者”）大约有多少人加入了淘金热？

options.en: nine million according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 九百万人（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states, "Back in the eastern states, gold fever didn't take hold until 1849. That year, ninety thousand people joined the gold rush. These were the forty-niners."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“在东部各州，淘金热直到1849年才兴起。那年，九万人加入了淘金热。这些人就是四九年淘金者。

q.en: Why does the author include the "Prices Gone Wild" chart showing a breakfast costing $43 in 1849?
q.zh: 作者为什么要收录“价格失控”图表，显示1849年一顿早餐要花43美元？

options.en: to explain how to cook in a mining camp
options.zh: 为了解释如何在矿工营地里做饭

options.en: to show that miners ate very healthy food according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了说明矿工吃的食物非常健康（按书中另一种说法）
为了将加利福尼亚与墨西哥作比较

options.en: to show that everyday goods were extremely expensive during the gold rush
options.zh: 为了说明淘金热期间日常商品价格极其昂贵

explain.en: The chart shows a breakfast cost $43 in 1849 (about $1,294 today), illustrating how steep prices were for everyday goods during the rush.
explain.zh: 图表显示1849年一顿早餐要43美元（约合今天1294美元），说明淘金热期间日常商品价格高得惊人。

q.en: How fast did most miners travel to California by wagon along the California Trail?
q.zh: 大多数矿工乘马车沿加州小道前往加利福尼亚时，速度大约是多少？

explain.en: The book says, "A wagon could go about 15 miles (24 km) a day" along the California Trail.
explain.zh: 书中写道：沿着加州小道，“一辆马车每天大约能走15英里（24公里）”。

q.en: According to the book, who suffered the worst during the gold rush?
q.zh: 根据本书，在淘金热中谁遭受的苦难最严重？

options.en: Native Americans, about 120,000 of whom died
options.zh: 美洲原住民，其中约12万人死亡

options.en: the forty-niners from the East Coast
options.zh: 来自东海岸的四九年淘金者

options.en: the foreign miners who paid a $20 tax according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 缴纳20美元税的外国矿工（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, "For Native Americans, the gold rush was awful... In California, about 120,000 Native Americans died of illness, starvation, or murder during the gold rush."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“对美洲原住民来说，淘金热是可怕的……在加利福尼亚，约12万美洲原住民在淘金热期间死于疾病、饥饿或谋杀。

q.en: The book explains that the word "prospector" comes from the gold rush and means a person with the ___ of finding gold.
q.zh: 本书解释说，“prospector”（探矿者）这个词源于淘金热，指的是一个怀有找到黄金的___的人。

options.en: treaty according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 条约（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, "It means a person who moves to a spot with the prospect, or possibility, of finding gold and making a fortune."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“它指的是一个搬到某地、怀有（prospect，即可能性）找到黄金并发财希望的人。

q.en: What does the author say was the biggest result, or "winner," of the gold rush?
q.zh: 作者说淘金热最大的结果或“赢家”是什么？

options.en: the forty-niners who found gold according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 那些找到黄金的四九年淘金者（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: the United States, which gained California as a state and grew its population
options.zh: 美国，它使加利福尼亚成为州并增加了人口

explain.en: The book states, "The biggest winner was the United States. The gold rush brought about three hundred thousand people to California... In 1850, California became a state."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“最大的赢家是美国。淘金热在几年内为加利福尼亚带来了约30万人……1850年，加利福尼亚成为一个州。

prompt.en: Write a diary entry from the point of view of a forty-niner in 1849 describing the journey and what you hope to find in California.
prompt.zh: 以1849年“四九年淘金者”的视角写一则日记，描述旅途以及你希望在加州找到什么。

en: Describe the hard journey by ship around South America or by wagon on the California Trail.
Explain your hope of making a fortune and returning home.
zh: 描述乘船绕行南美洲或乘马车走加州小道的艰苦旅程。
说明你希望发财并回家的愿望。
